title: The Glass Maker
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A master glass blower in 17th-century Venice must choose between his art and his life when the Inquisition comes for his secret technique.
long_description:
In 1658 Venice, Matteo Dandolo is the most celebrated glass blower on Murano, master of a furnace that has belonged to his family for three generations. He has discovered a technique for producing a ruby-red glass so pure and luminous that it seems lit from within — a color the Venetian Republic has pursued for decades. When the Inquisition learns that Matteo's formula involves a process bordering on heresy, they send Father Ambrogio to investigate.

As Matteo's world narrows from the wide lagoon to a prison cell, he must choose between surrendering his family's secret — the culmination of a lifetime's work — and risking everything to protect it. His assistant and muse, Elena Morosini, urges him to flee, but Matteo is rooted to his furnace, unable to imagine a life apart from his craft.

The Glass Maker is a story of art and faith, beauty and power, set against the glittering canals and smoky furnaces of 17th-century Venice.
main_characters: Matteo Dandolo — Master glass blower, late 30s, proud and guarded, widower whose secret ruby glass technique draws the Inquisition's attention
Elena Morosini — Matteo's assistant and muse, mid-20s, sharp and practical, the daughter of a glass merchant who knows the furnaces intimately
Father Ambrogio Contarini — Inquisitor of the Venetian Holy Office, early 50s, educated and pious, who believes the Church's authority is absolute
Doge Marco Pesaro — The Doge of Venice, who protects Murano's glass secrets but fears the Inquisition's reach
Maestro Lorenzo Fuseri — An older glass blower, Matteo's former master, bitter and nursing old grudges
Brother Michele — Ambrogio's young assistant monk, earnest and conflicted
Brother Anselmo — A Benedictine hermit and scholar of alchemical manuscripts
